as.doc: Marks a C# class as mapping to a specific Java class. The transpiler reads this to know what Java type to emit.

for.doc: Base class for custom items declared in C#. Extend this class, add [ItemTexture("texture_name")], and CSCraft automatically: - Registers the item in the Fabric item registry - Generates the item model JSON pointing to your texture - Copies the texture from Assets/textures/item/ into the mod jar Example: [ItemTexture("my_gem")] public class MyGem : CustomItem { public override string Id => "mymod:my_gem"; public override string Name => "Magic Gem"; public override ItemRarity Rarity => ItemRarity.Rare; public override void OnUse(McPlayer player, McWorld world) { world.CreateExplosion(player.X, player.Y, player.Z, 2.0f); } }

McBlock.doc: Facade for a Minecraft block type (Block / AbstractBlock). Use McRegistry.RegisterBlock() to create and register custom blocks. Transpiles to Java's Block.

McBlockEntity.doc: Facade for a Minecraft block entity (tile entity) such as chests, furnaces, etc. Wraps BlockEntity.

members.name: GetInventory
members.doc: Get the inventory of this block entity, if it has one. Returns null for non-inventory block entities.

McBlockPos.doc: Facade for a Minecraft block position (BlockPos). Transpiles to Java's BlockPos.

McBossBar.doc: Create and manage boss bars shown to players. Transpiles to Fabric's ServerBossBar.

McEnchantment.doc: Facade for Minecraft enchantments. Use to query enchantment levels on items. Transpiles to Java's Enchantment / EnchantmentHelper.

McEntity.doc: Facade for a Minecraft entity (Entity). Transpiles to Java's Entity.

McInventory.doc: Generic inventory manipulation (player inventory, ender chest, chests, etc.). Wraps Inventory / SimpleInventory.

members.name: Give
members.doc: Get a player's ender chest inventory (27 slots).</summary> [JavaMethod("{0}.getEnderChestInventory()")] public static McInventory EnderChest(McPlayer player) => null!; <summary> Add items to a player's inventory (same as GiveItem but with McItemStack). Returns true if all items were added, false if some were dropped.

members.name: Take
members.javaTemplate: { int _rem = {2}; for (int _i = 0; _i < {0}.getInventory().size() && _rem > 0; _i++) { var _s = {0}.getInventory().getStack(_i); if (!_s.isEmpty() && Registries.ITEM.getId(_s.getItem()).toString().equals({1})) { int _take = Math.min(_s.getCount(), _rem); _s.decrement(_take); _rem -= _take; } } }

McItem.doc: Facade for a Minecraft item type (Item). Use McRegistry.RegisterItem() to create and register custom items. Transpiles to Java's Item.

McItemStack.doc: Facade for a Minecraft item stack (ItemStack). Transpiles to Java's ItemStack.

McNbt.doc: Full NBT compound read/write for entities, block entities, and item stacks. Wraps NbtCompound.

McPlayer.doc: Facade for a Minecraft server player (ServerPlayerEntity). Transpiles to Java's ServerPlayerEntity.

McServer.doc: Facade for the Minecraft server (MinecraftServer). Transpiles to Java's MinecraftServer.

members.name: BanPlayer
members.javaTemplate: { var _profile = new com.mojang.authlib.GameProfile(null, {0}); var _entry = new net.minecraft.server.BannedPlayerEntry(_profile, null, null, null, {1}); {target}.getPlayerManager().getUserBanList().add(_entry); var _p = {target}.getPlayerManager().getPlayer({0}); if (_p != null) _p.networkHandler.disconnect(Text.literal({1})); }

McStatusEffect.doc: Facade for Minecraft status effects (StatusEffect / StatusEffectInstance). Use McRegistry.RegisterStatusEffect() for custom effects. Transpiles to Java's StatusEffect / StatusEffectInstance.

McWorld.doc: Facade for a Minecraft server world (ServerWorld). Transpiles to Java's ServerWorld.

members.name: SpawnEntity
members.javaTemplate: { var _et = net.minecraft.entity.EntityType.get({0}).orElse(null); if (_et != null) { var _ent = _et.create({target}); if (_ent != null) { _ent.setPosition({1},{2},{3}); {target}.spawnEntity(_ent); } } }

MyMod.doc: Place this attribute on your mod class (the one implementing IMod) to configure all Fabric template settings automatically. When you build, CSCraft reads these values and generates the entire FabricTemplate directory — no manual Gradle setup needed. Example: [ModInfo( Id = "mymod", Name = "My Cool Mod", Version = "1.0.0", Author = "YourName", Description = "A cool Fabric mod written in C#" )] public class MyMod : IMod { ... }

members.name: Grant
members.doc: Grant all criteria of an advancement to a player, completing it. advancementId example: "mymod:my_advancement"

members.name: Revoke
members.doc: Revoke (reset) an advancement for a player.

members.name: HasCompleted
members.doc: Check if a player has completed a specific advancement.

members.name: GrantCriterion
members.doc: Grant a specific criterion of an advancement (for multi-step advancements).

members.name: DropLoot
members.doc: Drop loot from a loot table at a world position (items are spawned as entities). tableId example: "minecraft:chests/simple_dungeon"
members.javaTemplate: { var _lootCtx = new net.minecraft.loot.context.LootContextParameterSet.Builder((ServerWorld){0}).add(net.minecraft.loot.context.LootContextParameters.ORIGIN, new net.minecraft.util.math.Vec3d({1},{2},{3})).build(net.minecraft.loot.context.LootContextTypes.CHEST); var _table = {0}.getServer().getReloadableRegistries().getLootTable(net.minecraft.loot.LootTable.EMPTY); _table.generateLoot(_lootCtx).forEach(s -> net.minecraft.entity.ItemEntity.spawn((ServerWorld){0}, new net.minecraft.util.math.BlockPos((int){1},(int){2},(int){3}), s)); }

members.name: GiveLootToPlayer
members.doc: Generate items from a loot table and give them to a player.

members.name: RunLater
members.doc: Run an action after a delay in ticks (20 ticks = 1 second). Example: McScheduler.RunLater(server, 100, s => s.Broadcast("10 seconds passed!"));

members.name: RunRepeating
members.doc: Run an action every intervalTicks, starting after the first interval. The action receives the server and a cancel handle — call cancel() to stop. Example: McScheduler.RunRepeating(server, 20, (s, cancel) => { ... });

members.name: RunAsync
members.doc: Run an action asynchronously (off the main thread). WARNING: Do NOT call Minecraft API from the async callback — use RunLater inside it to return to main thread.

members.name: IsInsideStructure
members.doc: Helpers for checking and generating structures in the world. Structure files are data-driven NBT in data/modid/structure/. Transpiles to StructureLocator / Structure calls. </summary> public static class McStructure { <summary> Check whether a block position is inside a structure. structureId example: "minecraft:village"

members.name: FindNearest
members.doc: Find the nearest structure of a type and return its block position, or null if none is found within search radius.

members.name: Place
members.doc: Place an NBT structure at a world position. structureId example: "mymod:my_house"
members.javaTemplate: { var _structManager = {0}.getServer().getStructureTemplateManager(); var _template = _structManager.getTemplateOrBlank(Identifier.of({4})); var _placement = new net.minecraft.structure.StructurePlacementData(); _template.place((ServerWorld){0}, new BlockPos({1},{2},{3}), new BlockPos({1},{2},{3}), _placement, {0}.getRandom(), 2); }
